Argued by Plaintiff. Argued. The Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT NRT LLC, NRT WEST, INC., VALLEY OF CALIFORNIA, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Plaintiffs shall file their amended complaint within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Plaintiffs' amended complaint shall be the sole operative complaint for the consolidated action. Plaintiffs, if necessary and proper, may file a motion for DOE Designation based on the operative complaint concurrently with the filing of the amended complaint. If such motion is filed, Defendants' time to respond will run from the date of notice of entry of order on the Doe Designation motion. If such motion is not filed, Defendants shall file their response in accordance with the law.
(a) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for breach of warranty of habitability and quiet enjoyment - non-opposition filed.
(b) Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action for UCL violation - improper remedy sought and no amendment suggested;
(c) Demurrer to the 6th, 9th, 16th, 17th, and 24th causes of action is sustained without leave to amend as they are barred by the applicable statute of limitations.
(d) Demurrer to the 11th to 15th; and 19th, 20th, causes of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendants including any events giving rise to the accrual of the cause of action and any facts giving rise to delayed discovery.
(e) Demurrer to the 22nd cause of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege in good faith each element of this cause of action based on events occurring on October 15, 2013.
The Court does not rule on the demurrer based on Statute of Limitations (possible DOE Amendment) as to causes of action 1, 2, 5, 9, 18 and 21 at this time. See Ruling RE: Plaintiff's Motion for Leave to Designate Does. Defendant may raise the same arguments is necessary and proper in their response to the amended complaint. Modified order to be submitted by prevailing party. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Mary Jackon, CSR#8688, 855-348-4997.(501)
